---
title: "Research"
---

Selected research themes. Detailed methods and code are kept in private
repositories; public summaries are provided here.

## Climate, temperature & COPD

Effects of extreme temperature, humidity, and climate variability on COPD
severity, morbidity, and mortality — spanning individual lung-function decline,
mediation analyses, and systematic reviews with meta-analysis.

## Air pollution & respiratory disease

PM2.5 and air pollution exposure in chronic respiratory disease, including the
heightened susceptibility of eosinophilic COPD and the pathways linking climate
and pollution to disease severity.

## Global & country-level mortality analyses

Country-level ecological analyses of how climate variability and air pollution
relate to respiratory and stroke mortality worldwide (2000–2020).

## Occupational & environmental exposure

Metal fume PM2.5 and lung-function decline among shipyard workers; volatile
organic compound exposure and respiratory symptoms among nail salon workers in
Vietnam.

## Current work at UBC

Environmental epidemiology using large Canadian cohorts — air pollution,
wildfire smoke, and the Canadian Longitudinal Study on Aging (CLSA).
